topics.article_summary: At least 8 U.S. nonprofit colleges have announced closures in 2026 (Siena Heights, Trinity Christian, Sterling, Providence Christian, Lourdes, Labouré, Hampshire, Anna Maria), and 6 mergers are in active transition, including New Jersey City University's merger into Kean University, which became fully effective July 1, 2026 (rebranded 'Kean Jersey City,' $25M in state transition funding). Since March 2020, ~48 nonprofit institutions have closed or announced closing, affecting an estimated 52,600+ students (Higher Ed Dive / BestColleges tracking). Huron Consulting projects up to a quarter of the country's ~1,700 private nonprofit four-year colleges could close or merge within a decade. Sector-wide financial strain is broader than small colleges alone: Moody's projects 2026 revenue growth (3.5%) trailing cost growth (4.4%), and even Yale is cutting its budget over a new $300M annual tax bill.

marketing_points.self_relevance: High — directly relevant to students at (or applying to) small tuition-dependent private colleges, where closure/merger risk is concentrated.
marketing_points.comparison_potential: High — school-specific: is your exact school on the closure or merger list, and how does that compare to peers.
marketing_points.decision_usefulness: High — affects enrollment decisions for prospective/transfer students and loan-discharge decisions for currently enrolled students at closing schools (120-day federal discharge window).
marketing_points.share_trigger: Medium-high — natural to send to a friend at, or applying to, a small private school.
marketing_points.comment_trigger: High — 'is your school on this list' invites direct comparison comments.

title_options.one_line_summary: Eight nonprofit colleges have shut down this year — here's the full list and what it means if you're at a small private school.

title_options.one_line_summary: New Jersey City University's merger into Kean University became fully effective today — most students only found out after it was official.

title_options.title: The $25M Merger Nobody Explained to Students
title_options.one_line_summary: A year-old merger deal just went live with $25 million in state funding, and barely anyone at the schools involved knew the details.

title_options.title: 14 Schools Are Disappearing in 2026 — Closures + Mergers, By the Numbers
title_options.one_line_summary: Between 8 closures and 6 mergers, 14 U.S. colleges are losing their independent identity this year.

topics.selected_title: 8 Colleges Just Closed in 2026 — Is Yours Safe?

slide_structure.cover_title: 8 Colleges Just Closed in 2026 — Is Yours Safe?
slide_structure.cover_subcopy: One merger became official today. A dozen more schools are quietly shutting down or being absorbed — here's the full list.

slide_structure.headline: As of today, New Jersey City University officially stopped existing as its own school.
body_bullets: Its merger into Kean University became fully effective July 1, rebranding the campus as "Kean Jersey City."
The state is funding the transition with $25 million — but the deal was signed over a year before most students knew the details.

slide_structure.headline: 8 colleges are closing this year, 6 more are merging, and 2025 saw 16 closures before that.
body_bullets: Three of the eight 2026 closures are small Massachusetts liberal arts colleges — Labouré, Hampshire, and Anna Maria.
Nearly every closing school saw enrollment drop 30–70% over the last decade.

slide_structure.headline: Huron Consulting projects up to 1 in 4 private nonprofit colleges could close or merge within a decade.
body_bullets: Since March 2020, about 48 nonprofit colleges have already closed or announced closing — affecting 52,600+ students.
Even Yale is cutting its budget over a new $300M annual tax bill. This isn't just a small-school problem.
slide_structure.source: Huron Consulting, via The College Investor & Higher Ed Dive

slide_structure.headline: You have 120 days after an official closure date to qualify for federal loan discharge.
body_bullets: If you accept a teach-out transfer to finish your degree elsewhere, you forfeit that discharge option.
Most 2026 closures already lined up teach-out partners — check your school's plan before you decide.
slide_structure.source: U.S. Department of Education rules, via The College Investor

slide_structure.headline: Is your school on any 'financial watch' list — or merging with another school? Drop what you've heard below.

discussion_questions: Is your school on any financial watch list, or have you heard rumors of a merger?
If your school merged with a bigger university tomorrow, would you even find out before it hit the news?
Would you rather your struggling school merge into a bigger name, or shut down completely?
Does knowing about closures like this change which schools you'd apply to or recommend?
